_doc: Template: builder + destroyer self-play loop. Builder policy tries to complete the task; destroyer policy resets / messes up the scene so the next builder rollout starts from a fresh disturbance. Value model rates each rollout for success_when checks. Safety block defines collision-current bounds and N1/N2/N3 recovery. Underscore-prefixed keys (_doc, _comment_*) are inline annotations stripped by the validator.

robot._comment_type: Options: 'arxx5_bimanual', 'bi_piper_follower'. Self-play assumes a bimanual rig.

collection_meta._comment_operator_name: Primary human at the station (intervention + Ctrl+Esc).
collection_meta._comment_adversary_operator: Informational only — name of a distinct second human alternating roles. Leave "" when one person handles both roles (the common case).
collection_meta._comment_is_adversary: true: this run has a destroyer role.
collection_meta._comment_acting_arms: Aggregate arm coverage across all roles in this self-play session. Typically ['left', 'right'] because the two roles often use different arms (e.g. builder=right_hand_only, destroyer=left_hand_only). Per-role arm restriction is set independently via task_spec.roles.<role>.action_mask.

task_meta._comment_stages: Set to a stage label only when collecting a partial / recovery trajectory. Model identity belongs in inference.model_name, not here.

task_description: FILL_ME_IN — describe what builder and destroyer do.

roles._comment_roles: self_play requires >=2 roles. Builder completes the task; destroyer disturbs the scene. Names are free-form but 'builder' / 'destroyer' is the convention. Each role can carry its own policy_server, success_when, etc.

builder.prompt: FILL_ME_IN — builder imperative, optionally with {speed} placeholder driven by speed_variation.
builder._comment_max_time_s: Per-role timeout in seconds; this is the per-builder-attempt budget. self_play uses the role timeout, NOT recording.episode_time_s.
builder._comment_action_mask: Options: 'right_hand_only', 'left_hand_only', null. Limits which arm the policy may move during this role.
builder._comment_timeout_recovery: What to do on timeout. Options: 'recovery_prompt' (re-prompt the policy), 'force_human_takeover' (pause and wait for operator).
builder._comment_home_wait_before_end_s: Seconds to wait at home before declaring the role complete. Filter for transient at-home states.

speed_variation._comment_speed_variation: Per-episode {speed} placeholder rotates through [lower, upper] in steps of step_s. Use to expose the policy to different speed budgets across rollouts.

success_when._comment_success_when: Tuple of conditions that must ALL hold for the role to count as success. Checked once per tick after the role's grace window.
success_when._comment_at_home: Require the robot to be at the home pose (defined by task_spec.reset.reference_pose).
success_when._comment_value_gte: Require value_model output >= this number. Drop or set null to ignore.
success_when._comment_speed_threshold: Require joint velocity below this (rad/s). Filter for 'stably at home', not 'passing through home'.

success_when._comment_value_lte: Destroyer goal is to push value toward -1 (failure state for builder). Tighter bound = stricter disruption.

policy_server._comment_policy_server: Shared policy server (handles both roles). For split servers, move policy_server INSIDE each role and remove this top-level one.

prompts._comment_prompts: Optional. Live prompt-switching map shared across roles: pressing Ctrl+<key> during a role swaps the active runtime's prompt to the matching string on the next chunk. Keys MUST be a single character (digit or letter); multi-char keys are rejected at load. Three things to know: (1) Switches do NOT survive role boundaries — the next role starts on its own roles.<name>.prompt (with speed_variation applied), then you can Ctrl+<key> again. (2) The switch only affects the CURRENTLY-active role's policy_runtime; the other role keeps its task-spec prompt for when it next runs. (3) Safety-recovery prompts (N1/N2/timeout) STILL override your choice — safety wins — but the recovery transition is recorded in 'prompt_switches' tagged with its source so you can audit the full timeline. The active prompt at each tick lands in the dataset's per-frame 'task' column; the 'prompt_switches' ordered timeline ({step, t_s, prompt, source}) is appended to each episode in episodes.jsonl and also emitted to recording_logs/<ts>/self_play_events.jsonl as 'prompt_switch' events. Common pattern: keep a 'rescue' prompt on Ctrl+r for when the builder gets stuck without triggering full intervention.
prompts.1: FILL_ME_IN — variant of the builder prompt (e.g. a re-grasp phrasing).
prompts.2: FILL_ME_IN — variant of the destroyer prompt.
prompts.r: FILL_ME_IN — optional 'rescue' / recovery prompt the operator can swap to without entering intervention. Add or drop entries freely.

value_model._comment_value_model: Separate value-model server used by success_when checks. Often on the same box as the policy server but a different port.

reset._comment_reset: Home pose definition. Robot must be at this pose (within threshold) and stable (below speed_threshold) to count as 'home' for success_when.at_home.
reset._comment_reference_pose: Float[14] joint positions [l1..l7, r1..r7] for arxx5_bimanual (gripper as joint 7). Capture with `python -m lerobot.scripts.get_pose ...`.
reset._comment_threshold: Per-joint distance tolerance for 'at home' (rad for arms, gripper units for grippers).
reset._comment_home_wait_s: Seconds robot must remain at home before reset is considered complete.

safety._comment_safety: Collision detection + N1/N2/N3 recovery policy. Triggered when joint currents exceed bounds.

collision_current_bounds._comment_collision_current_bounds: {joint_index: [min, max]} (null = unbounded that side). Joint indices are 1-based per arx convention. Numbers are amperes; calibrate per arm.

recovery._comment_recovery: Tiered recovery: N1 = first attempt, N3 = give up and ask for human. threshold = consecutive collision events that escalate to this tier.

n1.prompt: Collision happened. Return to a safe position.

n3._comment_action: n3 has no prompt — at this point we always hand control to the human.

recording._comment_num_episodes: How many builder+destroyer pairs to record. For infer-only self-play (no dataset), set to a large number and rely on Ctrl+Esc.
recording._comment_episode_time_s: Vestigial in self_play (role.max_time_s is authoritative) but still type-checked.
recording._comment_reset_time_s: Between builder/destroyer pairs. Often 0 for self-play (no operator reset).

inference._comment_model_name: Free-form label identifying the checkpoint being served (e.g. 'seatbelt_recap_v1'). Lands in meta.info.infer_meta so episodes can be grouped by model.
inference._comment_inference_speedup: 1.5 is common in self_play to fit more rollouts per hour, but only safe if the policy was trained on a similar action rate.
inference._comment_persist_inference_log: When true, every inference call from every per-role policy_runtime is appended to <dataset>/meta/inference_log.parquet, tagged with episode_index and role ('builder' / 'destroyer'). Useful for self-play latency analysis and 'who-did-what' attribution. ~600 KB/min total (regardless of role count). Off by default.

intervention._comment_intervention: How long the operator has to take over / evacuate when a role times out and timeout_recovery=force_human_takeover. Defaults at INTERVENTION_DEFAULTS.
intervention._comment_waiting_intervention_time_s: Seconds to give the operator to grab the leader / spacemouse before the recorder considers intervention started.
intervention._comment_waiting_evacuation_time_s: After intervention ends, seconds to let the operator step back before policy resumes.
intervention._comment_pose_sync_duration_s: How long the leader needs to match the follower pose before takeover is accepted.
intervention._comment_leader_movement_timeout_s: If the operator hasn't moved the leader in this many seconds during intervention, abort and reset.

self_play._comment_max_total_time_s: Cap on total session wall time in seconds. null = no cap (stop only by num_episodes or Ctrl+Esc).
self_play._comment_infer_only: true → run the loop but DO NOT write any dataset (no recording, no parquet). Use for demos / latency benchmarks where you just want to see the policies play. When true the entire recording block is largely vestigial; set reset_time_s=0 and enable_log_say=false for an uninterrupted demo.
